_meta.description: Biblical genealogies - how people in the Bible connect together
_meta.note: Based strictly on scriptural records. Ages and relationships as stated in the text.
_meta.structure: Each person has id, name, father, mother, spouses, children, lifespan, references, and notes

persons.name: Adam
persons.notes: First man, created from dust. Named all the animals.

persons.name: Eve
persons.notes: First woman, formed from Adam's rib. Called 'mother of all living'.

persons.name: Cain
persons.notes: Firstborn of Adam. Farmer. Killed his brother Abel. Marked and exiled to land of Nod.

persons.name: Wife of Cain
persons.notes: Name not recorded in scripture.

persons.name: Abel
persons.notes: Second son of Adam. Shepherd. His offering was accepted by God. Killed by Cain.

persons.name: Seth
persons.notes: Born after Abel's death. Eve said God appointed him in place of Abel.

persons.name: Enosh
persons.notes: In his time, people began to call on the name of the LORD.

persons.name: Jared
persons.notes: Second longest-lived person recorded in scripture.

persons.name: Enoch
persons.notes: Walked with God and was not, for God took him. Did not see death.

persons.name: Methuselah
persons.meaning: Man of the dart/spear, or 'when he dies it shall come'
persons.notes: Longest-lived person in the Bible. Died in the year of the flood.

persons.name: Lamech
persons.notes: Named Noah saying 'This one will comfort us concerning our work and toil of our hands.'

persons.name: Noah
persons.notes: Found grace in the eyes of the LORD. Built the ark. Saved through the flood with his family.

persons.name: Wife of Noah
persons.notes: Name not recorded in scripture. Entered the ark with Noah.

persons.name: Enoch (son of Cain)
persons.notes: Not the same as Enoch son of Jared. Cain built a city named after him.

persons.name: Lamech (of Cain's line)
persons.notes: First recorded polygamist. Composed a poem about killing a man who wounded him.

persons.name: Adah
persons.notes: First wife of Lamech (Cain's line).

persons.name: Zillah
persons.notes: Second wife of Lamech (Cain's line).

persons.name: Jabal
persons.notes: Father of those who dwell in tents and have livestock.

persons.name: Jubal
persons.notes: Father of all who play the harp and flute.

persons.name: Tubal-Cain
persons.notes: Forger of all implements of bronze and iron.

persons.name: Naamah
persons.notes: Sister of Tubal-Cain. One of few women named in early Genesis.

persons.name: Shem
persons.notes: Eldest son of Noah. Ancestor of the Semitic peoples including Israel.

persons.name: Ham
persons.notes: Son of Noah. Saw his father's nakedness. Father of Canaan.

persons.name: Japheth
persons.notes: Son of Noah. Blessed to dwell in the tents of Shem.

persons.name: Arphaxad
persons.notes: Born 2 years after the flood.

persons.name: Eber
persons.notes: Ancestor of the Hebrews. Name may be origin of 'Hebrew'.

persons.name: Peleg
persons.notes: In his days the earth was divided (possibly referring to Babel).

persons.name: Joktan
persons.meaning: Small, he will be made little
persons.notes: Father of 13 sons. Brother of Peleg.

persons.name: Nahor (grandfather of Abraham)
persons.notes: Not the same as Nahor, Abraham's brother.

persons.name: Terah
persons.notes: Left Ur of the Chaldeans with Abraham. Died in Haran.

persons.name: Abraham
persons.meaning: Father of many nations (originally Abram: exalted father)
persons.notes: Called by God from Ur. Given covenant promises. Father of Isaac through Sarah, Ishmael through Hagar.

persons.name: Nahor (brother of Abraham)
persons.notes: Brother of Abraham. Stayed in Mesopotamia. Grandfather of Rebekah.

persons.name: Haran
persons.notes: Died in Ur before Terah. Father of Lot.

persons.name: Lot
persons.notes: Nephew of Abraham. Chose to live near Sodom. Escaped its destruction.

persons.name: Wife of Lot
persons.notes: Name not recorded. Looked back at Sodom and became a pillar of salt.

persons.name: Moab
persons.notes: Son of Lot by his firstborn daughter. Ancestor of the Moabites. Ruth was a Moabite.

persons.name: Ben-Ammi
persons.notes: Son of Lot by his younger daughter. Ancestor of the Ammonites.

persons.name: Milcah
persons.notes: Daughter of Haran, wife of Nahor. Grandmother of Rebekah and Laban.

persons.name: Bethuel
persons.notes: Father of Rebekah and Laban. Called 'the Aramean'.

persons.name: Elam
persons.notes: Son of Shem. Ancestor of the Elamites (in modern-day Iran).

persons.name: Asshur
persons.notes: Son of Shem. Ancestor of the Assyrians.

persons.name: Lud
persons.notes: Son of Shem. Ancestor of the Lydians.

persons.name: Aram
persons.notes: Son of Shem. Ancestor of the Arameans (Syrians).

persons.name: Cush
persons.notes: Son of Ham. Ancestor of the Cushites (Ethiopians).

persons.name: Nimrod
persons.notes: Mighty hunter before the LORD. His kingdom began with Babel, Erech, Accad, and Calneh in Shinar. Built Nineveh and other cities.

persons.name: Mizraim
persons.notes: Son of Ham. Ancestor of the Egyptians. Mizraim is the Hebrew name for Egypt.

persons.name: Put
persons.notes: Son of Ham. Ancestor of the Libyans/North Africans.

persons.name: Canaan
persons.notes: Son of Ham. Cursed by Noah because of Ham's actions. Ancestor of the Canaanite peoples.

persons.name: Gomer
persons.notes: Son of Japheth. Ancestor of peoples in Asia Minor.

persons.name: Magog
persons.notes: Son of Japheth. Associated with Gog in prophetic literature.

persons.name: Madai
persons.notes: Son of Japheth. Ancestor of the Medes.

persons.name: Javan
persons.notes: Son of Japheth. Ancestor of the Greeks and Ionians.

persons.name: Tubal
persons.notes: Son of Japheth. Mentioned with Meshech in Ezekiel.

persons.name: Meshech
persons.notes: Son of Japheth. Mentioned with Tubal in Ezekiel.

persons.name: Tiras
persons.notes: Son of Japheth. Possibly ancestor of Thracians.

persons.name: Sarah
persons.notes: Wife of Abraham, half-sister (same father). Barren until age 90. Mother of Isaac.

persons.name: Hagar
persons.notes: Egyptian servant of Sarah. Given to Abraham as wife. Mother of Ishmael.

persons.name: Keturah
persons.notes: Wife of Abraham after Sarah's death. Mother of 6 sons.

persons.name: Ishmael
persons.notes: Firstborn of Abraham through Hagar. Blessed to become a great nation. Father of 12 princes.

persons.name: Isaac
persons.notes: Son of promise. Bound on altar at Moriah. Husband of Rebekah.

persons.name: Midian
persons.notes: Son of Abraham by Keturah. Ancestor of the Midianites. Moses fled to Midian.

persons.name: Rebekah
persons.notes: Granddaughter of Nahor (Abraham's brother). Barren for 20 years. Mother of twins Esau and Jacob.

persons.name: Laban
persons.notes: Brother of Rebekah. Father-in-law of Jacob. Known for deception.

persons.name: Esau
persons.notes: Firstborn twin. Sold birthright for stew. Father of the Edomites.

persons.name: Jacob
persons.meaning: Supplanter, heel-grabber (renamed Israel: prince with God)
persons.notes: Younger twin. Wrestled with God at Peniel. Father of the 12 tribes of Israel.

persons.name: Leah
persons.notes: First wife of Jacob (by Laban's deception). Mother of 6 sons and 1 daughter. Buried in Machpelah.

persons.name: Rachel
persons.notes: Beloved wife of Jacob. Barren until Joseph. Died giving birth to Benjamin near Bethlehem.

persons.name: Bilhah
persons.notes: Rachel's servant. Given to Jacob as wife. Mother of Dan and Naphtali.

persons.name: Zilpah
persons.notes: Leah's servant. Given to Jacob as wife. Mother of Gad and Asher.

persons.name: Reuben
persons.notes: Firstborn of Jacob. Lost birthright due to sin with Bilhah. Tried to save Joseph.

persons.name: Simeon
persons.notes: Second son of Jacob. With Levi, massacred Shechem. Held hostage in Egypt.

persons.name: Levi
persons.notes: Third son of Jacob. With Simeon, massacred Shechem. Ancestor of the priestly tribe.

persons.name: Judah
persons.notes: Fourth son of Jacob. Received the blessing of rulership. Ancestor of David and Jesus.

persons.name: Dan
persons.notes: Fifth son of Jacob. First son through Bilhah.

persons.name: Naphtali
persons.notes: Sixth son of Jacob. Second son through Bilhah.

persons.name: Gad
persons.notes: Seventh son of Jacob. First son through Zilpah.

persons.name: Asher
persons.notes: Eighth son of Jacob. Second son through Zilpah.

persons.name: Issachar
persons.notes: Ninth son of Jacob. Fifth son through Leah.

persons.name: Zebulun
persons.notes: Tenth son of Jacob. Sixth son through Leah.

persons.name: Dinah
persons.notes: Only named daughter of Jacob. Defiled by Shechem, leading to Simeon and Levi's vengeance.

persons.name: Joseph
persons.notes: Eleventh son of Jacob. First son through Rachel. Sold into slavery. Rose to rule Egypt under Pharaoh.

persons.name: Benjamin
persons.meaning: Son of the right hand (originally Ben-Oni: son of my sorrow)
persons.notes: Twelfth and youngest son of Jacob. Rachel died giving him birth.

persons.name: Asenath
persons.notes: Daughter of Potiphera, priest of On. Given to Joseph as wife by Pharaoh.

persons.name: Manasseh
persons.notes: Firstborn of Joseph. Received lesser blessing when Jacob crossed his hands.

persons.name: Ephraim
persons.notes: Second son of Joseph. Received the greater blessing from Jacob.

persons.name: Tamar
persons.notes: Canaanite woman. Wife of Er, then by levirate custom, mother of Perez and Zerah by Judah. In lineage of Jesus.

persons.name: Er
persons.notes: Firstborn of Judah. Was wicked in sight of the LORD, who put him to death.

persons.name: Onan
persons.notes: Second son of Judah. Refused levirate duty. Put to death by the LORD.

persons.name: Shelah (son of Judah)
persons.notes: Third son of Judah. Withheld from Tamar.

persons.name: Perez
persons.notes: Twin son of Judah and Tamar. Broke through first in birth. In lineage of David and Jesus.

persons.name: Zerah
persons.notes: Twin son of Judah and Tamar. Scarlet thread tied on his hand.

persons.name: Daughter of Shua
persons.notes: Canaanite woman. First wife of Judah. Name not recorded.

persons.name: Hezron
persons.notes: Son of Perez. In lineage of David and Jesus.

persons.name: Ram
persons.notes: Son of Hezron. In lineage of David and Jesus.

persons.name: Amminadab
persons.notes: Father of Nahshon, leader of Judah in the wilderness. Father-in-law of Aaron.

persons.name: Elisheba
persons.notes: Sister of Nahshon. Wife of Aaron the high priest.

persons.name: Nahshon
persons.notes: Leader of the tribe of Judah during the Exodus. Brother-in-law of Aaron.

persons.name: Salmon
persons.notes: Married Rahab of Jericho. In lineage of David and Jesus.

persons.name: Rahab
persons.notes: Canaanite woman of Jericho. Hid the Israelite spies. Her family was spared. In lineage of Jesus.

persons.name: Boaz
persons.notes: Wealthy landowner in Bethlehem. Kinsman-redeemer who married Ruth.

persons.name: Ruth
persons.notes: Moabite woman. Widow of Mahlon. Chose to follow Naomi and the God of Israel. Great-grandmother of David.

persons.name: Obed
persons.notes: Son of Boaz and Ruth. Grandfather of David.

persons.name: Jesse
persons.notes: Bethlehemite. Father of 8 sons including David. Called 'the Ephrathite'.

persons.name: David
persons.notes: Shepherd, warrior, king. Man after God's own heart. Slew Goliath. Second king of Israel. Received covenant of eternal throne.

persons.name: Bathsheba
persons.notes: Wife of Uriah the Hittite, then of David. Mother of Solomon.

persons.name: Solomon
persons.meaning: Peace (also called Jedidiah: beloved of the LORD)
persons.notes: Third king of Israel. Built the Temple. Wisest man. Had 700 wives and 300 concubines. His heart turned away in old age.

persons.name: Nathan (son of David)
persons.notes: Son of David through Bathsheba. Ancestor of Jesus through Mary (Luke's genealogy).

persons.name: Rehoboam
persons.notes: Son of Solomon. First king of Judah after the kingdom split. Rejected elders' counsel.

persons.name: Abijah
persons.notes: King of Judah. Defeated Jeroboam in battle.

persons.name: Asa
persons.notes: King of Judah. Did what was right. Removed idols. Relied on God against Ethiopia.

persons.name: Jehoshaphat
persons.notes: King of Judah. Good king who sent teachers throughout Judah. Allied with Ahab.

persons.name: Jehoram (of Judah)
persons.notes: King of Judah. Did evil. Married Athaliah, daughter of Ahab. Killed his brothers.

persons.name: Ahaziah (of Judah)
persons.notes: King of Judah. Did evil. Killed by Jehu along with Joram of Israel.

persons.name: Joash (of Judah)
persons.notes: King of Judah. Hidden as infant from Athaliah. Restored the Temple. Later turned to idols.

persons.name: Amaziah
persons.notes: King of Judah. Did right but not with whole heart. Defeated Edom but then worshipped their gods.

persons.name: Uzziah
persons.meaning: My strength is the LORD (also called Azariah)
persons.notes: King of Judah. Prospered greatly. Struck with leprosy for burning incense in the Temple.

persons.name: Jotham
persons.notes: King of Judah. Did right. Built the upper gate of the Temple.

persons.name: Ahaz
persons.notes: King of Judah. Did evil. Made his son pass through fire. Asked Assyria for help against Syria and Israel.

persons.name: Hezekiah
persons.notes: King of Judah. Did right. Cleansed the Temple. Trusted God against Assyria. Given 15 more years.

persons.name: Manasseh (king of Judah)
persons.notes: King of Judah. Reigned 55 years (longest). Did much evil. Later repented in captivity.

persons.name: Amon
persons.notes: King of Judah. Did evil. Assassinated by his servants after 2 years.

persons.name: Josiah
persons.notes: King of Judah. Did right. Found the Book of the Law. Great reforms. Killed at Megiddo.

persons.name: Jehoiakim
persons.notes: King of Judah. Made king by Pharaoh Neco. Did evil. Burned Jeremiah's scroll.

persons.name: Jehoiachin
persons.meaning: The LORD establishes (also called Jeconiah, Coniah)
persons.notes: King of Judah. Reigned 3 months. Taken captive to Babylon. Later released and honored.

persons.name: Shealtiel
persons.notes: Son of Jehoiachin. Father of Zerubbabel (or legal father).

persons.name: Zerubbabel
persons.notes: Governor of Judah. Led first return from Babylon. Rebuilt the Temple foundation.

persons.name: Abiud
persons.notes: Son of Zerubbabel. In lineage of Jesus (Matthew).

persons.name: Eliakim (son of Abiud)
persons.notes: In lineage of Jesus (Matthew).

persons.name: Azor
persons.notes: In lineage of Jesus (Matthew).

persons.name: Zadok (son of Azor)
persons.notes: In lineage of Jesus (Matthew).

persons.name: Achim
persons.notes: In lineage of Jesus (Matthew).

persons.name: Eliud
persons.notes: In lineage of Jesus (Matthew).

persons.name: Eleazar (son of Eliud)
persons.notes: In lineage of Jesus (Matthew).

persons.name: Matthan
persons.notes: In lineage of Jesus (Matthew). Grandfather of Joseph.

persons.name: Jacob (father of Joseph)
persons.notes: Father of Joseph, husband of Mary.

persons.name: Joseph (husband of Mary)
persons.notes: Descendant of David. Carpenter. Legal father of Jesus. Righteous man.

persons.name: Mary (mother of Jesus)
persons.notes: Virgin betrothed to Joseph. Conceived by Holy Spirit. Mother of Jesus.

persons.name: Jesus
persons.meaning: The LORD saves (Greek form of Joshua)
persons.notes: The Christ, Son of God. Born of the virgin Mary. Descendant of David through Joseph (legally) and Mary (biologically).

persons.name: James (brother of Jesus)
persons.notes: Brother of Jesus. Leader of the Jerusalem church. Author of the epistle of James.

persons.name: Gershon
persons.notes: Firstborn of Levi. His descendants cared for tabernacle coverings and hangings.

persons.name: Kohath
persons.notes: Second son of Levi. His descendants carried the holy vessels of the tabernacle.

persons.name: Merari
persons.notes: Third son of Levi. His descendants cared for tabernacle frames and pillars.

persons.name: Amram
persons.notes: Father of Moses, Aaron, and Miriam. Married his father's sister Jochebed.

persons.name: Jochebed
persons.notes: Daughter of Levi. Wife of Amram. Mother of Moses, Aaron, and Miriam.

persons.name: Miriam
persons.notes: Sister of Moses and Aaron. Prophetess. Led women in song after Red Sea. Struck with leprosy for opposing Moses.

persons.name: Aaron
persons.notes: First high priest. Spokesman for Moses. Made the golden calf. Died on Mount Hor.

persons.name: Moses
persons.notes: Deliverer of Israel. Received the Law at Sinai. Led Israel 40 years in wilderness. Did not enter Promised Land.

persons.name: Zipporah
persons.notes: Daughter of Jethro, priest of Midian. Wife of Moses. Circumcised their son.

persons.name: Jethro
persons.notes: Priest of Midian. Father-in-law of Moses. Advised Moses on delegating judgments.

persons.name: Nadab (son of Aaron)
persons.notes: Firstborn of Aaron. Died before the LORD when he offered strange fire.

persons.name: Abihu
persons.notes: Second son of Aaron. Died with Nadab for offering strange fire.

persons.name: Eleazar
persons.notes: Third son of Aaron. Became high priest after Aaron. Buried at Gibeah.

persons.name: Ithamar
persons.notes: Fourth son of Aaron. Oversaw Gershonite and Merarite Levites.

persons.name: Phinehas
persons.notes: Grandson of Aaron. Zealous for God at Peor. Given covenant of perpetual priesthood.

persons.name: Eli
persons.notes: High priest and judge of Israel. Raised Samuel. His sons were wicked. Died when ark was captured.

persons.name: Samuel
persons.notes: Prophet and last judge of Israel. Anointed Saul and David as kings. Dedicated to God from birth.

persons.name: Hannah
persons.notes: Barren wife of Elkanah. Prayed for a son and vowed to dedicate him. Mother of Samuel.

persons.name: Zadok (high priest)
persons.notes: High priest under David and Solomon. Faithful during Absalom's rebellion. Anointed Solomon.

persons.name: Zechariah (father of John the Baptist)
persons.notes: Priest of the division of Abijah. Made mute for doubting Gabriel. Father of John the Baptist.

persons.name: Elizabeth
persons.notes: Descendant of Aaron. Relative of Mary. Barren until old age. Mother of John the Baptist.

persons.name: John the Baptist
persons.notes: Forerunner of Jesus. Preached repentance. Baptized Jesus. Beheaded by Herod.

adam-jesus.name: Line of Promise (Matthew)
adam-jesus.description: The genealogical line from Adam to Jesus through Seth, Shem, Abraham, Judah, David, and Solomon (Matthew's genealogy through Joseph)

adam-jesus-luke.name: Line of Promise (Luke)
adam-jesus-luke.description: The genealogical line from Adam to Jesus through David's son Nathan (Luke's genealogy, possibly through Mary)

cain.name: Line of Cain
cain.description: Descendants of Cain. This line ends at the flood.

shem.name: Descendants of Shem
shem.description: Semitic peoples descended from Shem but not in the direct line to Abraham

ham.name: Descendants of Ham
ham.description: Peoples descended from Ham: Cush (Ethiopia), Mizraim (Egypt), Put (Libya), Canaan

japheth.name: Descendants of Japheth
japheth.description: Peoples descended from Japheth: Greeks, Medes, coastal and island nations

ishmael.name: Line of Ishmael
ishmael.description: Descendants of Abraham through Hagar. Father of 12 princes and the Arab peoples.

abraham-keturah.name: Sons of Keturah
abraham-keturah.description: Descendants of Abraham through Keturah, including Midian

esau.name: Line of Esau (Edom)
esau.description: Descendants of Esau, the Edomites. Includes the Herodian dynasty.

israel.name: Tribes of Israel
israel.description: The 12 tribes descended from Jacob's sons (excluding Judah, Levi, and Joseph which have their own lines)

judah.name: Tribe of Judah
judah.description: Descendants of Judah not in the direct royal line

levi.name: Tribe of Levi (Priests)
levi.description: Levites and the priestly line through Aaron

joseph.name: Tribes of Joseph
joseph.description: Descendants of Joseph through Ephraim and Manasseh
